# Vendors Overview: Printing and Shipping Setup (Printify-Style POD)

## Purpose

This document outlines the practical steps to set up print-on-demand (POD) printing and shipping operations with vendors like Printify.

## Important Note

Platform features and vendor terms change over time. Confirm all pricing, SLA, and policy details directly in your vendor dashboard and contract terms.

## 1. Define Vendor Requirements Before You Choose

Set your must-have criteria first so you can evaluate vendors objectively.

- Product catalog fit (shirt styles, blanks, sizes, colors)
- Print methods offered (DTG, DTF, screen print where applicable)
- Production time SLA and holiday performance
- Shipping coverage (domestic/international) and tracking quality
- Branding options (inside labels, pack-ins, custom packaging)
- Return/reprint policy and support responsiveness
- Ecommerce platform integrations (Shopify, Etsy, etc.)
- Cost structure and margin fit

## 2. Create and Configure Your Vendor Account

For platforms like Printify:

1. Create a business account.
2. Complete billing and payout settings.
3. Set default currency and tax settings.
4. Connect your ecommerce store (for example Shopify).
5. Enable order sync and verify API/app permissions.

## 3. Select Print Providers (Within the Platform)

Many POD platforms use multiple print providers. Choose intentionally.

- Compare providers by product cost, print quality ratings, and location.
- Prioritize providers closer to your target customers for faster shipping.
- Keep at least one backup provider for best-selling SKUs.

## 4. Build Product Templates Correctly

For each shirt product:

- Choose blank brand/model and colorways.
- Upload artwork at required resolution and color profile.
- Confirm print area boundaries and safe margins.
- Generate realistic mockups for product pages.
- Set SKU naming conventions for easy troubleshooting.

## 5. Price for Margin and Ad Spend

Use a pricing model that covers:

- Base product cost
- Printing cost (if separate)
- Shipping cost (single and multi-item)
- Platform/payment fees
- Target contribution margin after ad spend

### Basic Margin Check
- Target gross margin range: typically 45%-60% for POD t-shirts before ad spend.
- Review margins by variant; do not assume all colors/sizes cost the same.

## 6. Configure Shipping Settings End-to-End

- Choose shipping profiles/rates in your storefront.
- Match storefront promises to real vendor production + transit times.
- Decide free shipping threshold strategy (for example free shipping over $60).
- Configure tracking notification automation.
- Define international shipping rules and excluded regions.

## 7. Quality Control Before Launch

Run sample orders before accepting live customers.

- Order 2-3 samples per hero design across different sizes/colors.
- Check print alignment, color consistency, fabric feel, and wash durability.
- Test packaging quality and tracking email flow.
- Record approved provider/SKU combinations.

## 8. Publish Customer-Facing Policy Stack

Your storefront should clearly publish:

- Production time expectations
- Shipping windows by region
- Return/replacement policy for damaged/misprinted items
- Support contact method and response times

## 9. Launch Workflow and Daily Operations

## Daily
- Monitor failed/sync-error orders.
- Check in-production and delayed shipments.
- Resolve customer support tickets quickly.

## Weekly
- Review provider performance (on-time rate, defect rate).
- Audit profit margins by top SKUs.
- Compare provider alternatives for top sellers.

## Monthly
- Re-negotiate or re-select providers if SLA/performance slips.
- Refresh product line based on sell-through and return data.

## 10. Exception Handling SOP (Recommended)

Create a simple internal SOP for:

- Misprints and damaged items
- Lost shipments
- Late delivery escalations
- Address change requests
- Replacement/refund decision rules

## 11. KPI Dashboard for Vendor Operations

Track these metrics:

- Order defect rate
- Reprint/replacement rate
- On-time shipment rate
- Average production time
- Average delivery time
- Shipping cost per order
- Gross margin by SKU/provider
- Customer complaint rate

## 12. Risk Mitigation Checklist

- Keep backup print providers pre-approved.
- Avoid single-point dependency on one vendor for all hero products.
- Maintain design files and product mappings in a shared folder.
- Test peak-season timelines early.
- Keep emergency customer communication templates ready.

## Quick Start Checklist (First 14 Days)

1. Define vendor scorecard criteria.
2. Set up Printify (or equivalent) account and store integration.
3. Select primary and backup print providers.
4. Build top 10-20 products with clean SKUs.
5. Place sample orders and approve only passing combinations.
6. Configure shipping profiles and policy pages.
7. Launch with monitoring dashboard and support SOP live.
